summary: M2 KBA (Knowledge-Based Authentication) backend + frontend tested end-to-end. All 17 pytest cases passed and the full UI flow (status -> launch -> modal -> start -> 5-question quiz with timer -> submit -> result -> last-result card) works correctly using the MockKBAProvider. correct_id is not leaked in API responses or the DOM. Rate limiting (2/24h with 429), ownership checks (403), unknown session (404), already-completed (400), admin fraud-signals (403 for non-admin / 200 for admin), and sanitized session GET (no _questions_internal) all behave per spec. No regressions observed on FL state-profile endpoint.
critical_code_review_comments: kba_routes.py uses pydantic v1 style 'min_items=1' on SubmitKBAReq.answers — works for now but will warn under pydantic v2 (prefer Field(min_length=1)).
Submit endpoint accepts any number of answers; consider validating answers length == questions_count (or warning) for robustness.
Mock questions are deterministic per user — same correct answer every retry. Acceptable for MOCK provider per spec, but document so QA doesn't farm correct answers across runs.
Server-side fingerprint is UA+Accept-Language only (32-char sha). Sufficient for fraud correlation but document the limitation vs FingerprintJS.
test_credentials: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123!  |  demo@test.com / Demo123!  |  notarytest@test.com / Test123!
seed_data_creation: Test run consumed all 2/24h KBA attempts for notarytest@test.com (via rate-limit test) and 1 attempt for admin@notarychain.com (via UI flow). Demo user untouched in this iteration (already had 1 from prior).
context_for_next_testing_agent: notarytest@test.com is fully rate-limited (2/2) and admin has 1 attempt used. demo had 1 from prior iteration. If retesting starts/submits within 24h, prefer fresh users or wait 24h. Backend test_kba.py uses pytest.<attr> to share session_id across tests in the same module run — safe in a single pytest invocation but not parallel-safe.
